20.reason: [Error: This condition is incorrect. It should print a newline only after printing all k numbers, i.e., after the loop ends, not inside the loop based on a%k==2. The intended output is one permutation per line, but this logic will print newlines at wrong positions and may cause extra spaces or missing newlines.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: This condition is incorrect. It should print a newline only after printing all k numbers, i.e., after the loop ends, not inside the loop based on a%k==2. The intended output is one permutation per line, but this logic will print newlines at wrong positions and may cause extra spaces or missing newlines.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: This condition is incorrect. It should print a newline after every k elements (i.e., after each permutation), but a%k==2 only triggers when a is 2, 5, 8, etc., which is not the correct logic. The correct condition should be after the loop ends or use a separate check to print newline only once per permutation.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The condition for printing newline is incorrect. It should print a newline only after all numbers are printed, not based on a modulo condition. This results in missing newlines for many k values and incorrect line breaks for others. The correct approach is to print a newline after the loop.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  Logical error: Incorrect condition for newline insertion. A newline should be printed once after all k numbers are output, not based on modulo 2.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  Incorrect condition for printing newline; it prevents the newline from being output correctly after each permutation. printf("\n"); should be executed once after the loop finishes.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  // This condition `a%k==2` is incorrect and causes extra newlines; it should not print newline inside the loop since each permutation must be on its own line. Also, this leads to undefined behavior when k<=2 because `a%k` can never be 2.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  // This condition `a%k==2` is incorrect and causes extra newlines. It should not print newline inside the loop; instead, a single newline should be printed after the entire permutation.

17.reason: Compilation error: variable declaration inside for loop is not allowed in C89/C90; causes compilation failure.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  Compilation error: variable declaration inside for loop is not allowed in C89/C90; causes compilation failure.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  Compilation error: C89/C90 standard does not allow declaring loop variables inside the for statement. Declare 'int a;' before the loop.

19.reason: Logical/Semantic error: Using char array and %c cannot correctly store/print the number 10, which requires two characters.

29.reason: Semantic error: When i=10, '0'+10 results in ':', not "10". A char array cannot correctly represent two-digit numbers.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  Semantic error: When i=10, '0'+10 results in ':', not "10". A char array cannot correctly represent two-digit numbers.
